title: When Dealing with Misinformation: - Fact-Check: Verify the Source of Any Information Before Accepting
subtitle: Combat the Continued Influence Effect
description:
When dealing with misinformation:
- Fact-check: Verify the source of any information before accepting it.
- Mentally replace: Once corrected, actively focus on the updated information.
- Repeat the truth: Reinforce the correct version to overwrite the false one.

Example: If a rumor about a coworker turns out false, remind yourself of the actual facts whenever you think about them.

howTo.title: Verify the source of information
howTo.summary: Take 5-10 minutes to check the claim's origin. Look for official statements or reports from at least two reputable outlets. This helps prevent the spread of misinformation.

howTo.title: Correct the misinformation
howTo.summary: If the claim is false, write a one-sentence correction and a plausible alternative explanation. This helps replace the false narrative with accurate information.

howTo.title: Reinforce the correct information
howTo.summary: Schedule two rehearsals within 48 hours to repeat the corrected fact. This repetition aids in reducing the cognitive stickiness of the misinformation.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your verification results and rehearsals in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/continued-influence-effect-misinformation/.
